topic.what: Improved decoding failure rate (DFR) model for HQC key encapsulation mechanism
topic.how: Eliminates independence assumptions on error vector coordinates and derives exact closed-form DFR model for concatenated Reed-Solomon and Duplicated Reed-Muller code decoder
topic.why_matters: DFR must be ≤2^-λ for IND-CCA2 security; incorrect models lead to insecure parameters or wasteful over-provisioning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Probability of decryption failure in HQC
characteristics.vs_baseline: Previous model was conservative by up to 29 bits
characteristics.evidence: Table I with closed-form model; validated against 10^6 simulations
characteristics.context: Conservative margins of ~19-29 bits across all HQC parameter sets

characteristics.what_itasures: Size of HQC public key in bytes
characteristics.value: 2206 vs 2241 for HQC-1 (-35 bytes); 4507 vs 4514 for HQC-3 (-7 bytes); 7144 vs 7237 for HQC-5 (-93 bytes)
characteristics.evidence: Table I; optimized parameters maintain ≥128-bit security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Size of HQC ciphertext in bytes
characteristics.value: 4302 vs 4433 for HQC-1 (-131 bytes); 8811 vs 8978 for HQC-3 (-167 bytes); 14168 vs 14421 for HQC-5 (-253 bytes)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bits of security margin against message/key recovery attacks
characteristics.value: 0.2 bits loss for optimized parameters; ~19-29 bits for original parameters
characteristics.evidence: Table II using complexity estimators from [14]

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fit of DFR model to empirical distribution vs binary symmetric channel approximation
characteristics.value: Non-negligible statistical distance between real and BSC-approximated error weight distribution
characteristics.evidence: Figure 1a shows better fit to empirical distribution from 10^8 samples
characteristics.context: Exact model uses Fisher's non-central hypergeometric distribution

characteristics.what_itasures: Time to compute DFR estimates for parameter tuning
characteristics.value: Reduced from days to few minutes using optimization techniques
characteristics.evidence: Section III-D optimization strategy with iterative interpolation

requires: Access to computational resources for parameter search

novelty: Exact characterization of error weight distribution without independence assumptions
Closed-form DFR model using Fisher's non-central hypergeometric distribution
Sequential DRM decoding model capturing conditional failure dependencies
Optimization techniques enabling DFR model as parameter tuning tool
Verified that HQC's original model remains conservative in cryptographic regime
open_problems: Extension to different error patterns or modified HQC variants
More aggressive parameter reductions with different DFR targets
Trade-offs between DFR and decoding performance
